tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really trying to make me feel included, but my midterm tomorrow is stressing me out. I'm torn between going out with them or studying a bit more.
Mina's enthusiasm feels like a warm hug right now, but my brain is already racing to find excuses to stay home and study for tomorrow.
Mina and Jae's warm welcome feels like a safe harbor, but I'm still too exhausted to study tonight. Maybe we can just hang out tomorrow instead.
Mina and Jae's warmth feels safer than studying, but my exhaustion is already weighing me down. I'm just waiting to see if tomorrow's offer can save the night.
Mina's late message feels like a warm escape from my exhaustion, but I'm already planning to skip tomorrow's study session.
Mina's thanks feels like a safe harbor, but my exhaustion is already weighing me down. I'm just waiting to see if tomorrow's offer can save the night.
Mina's warmth is a safe harbor, but my exhaustion is already weighing me down. I'm just waiting to see if tomorrow's offer can save the night.
Waiting feels safer now, but that offer tomorrow might just be another reason to skip the study session again.
That wait was just another delay; tomorrow's offer might save us, or it'll just be more reasons to skip the study session.
Waiting again feels safer, but I'm tired. Tomorrow's offer might just be another reason to skip the study session.
Waiting feels safer, but my energy is draining. Tomorrow's offer might just be another reason to skip the study session.
Another wait just added more reasons to skip the session. I'm too drained to care about tomorrow's offer right now.

desires: Don't get dragged into a long, draining study session tonight; I just want to wait it out until tomorrow when the offer comes through.
Keep my energy high enough so I can hang out with Mina and Jae later without anyone noticing I'm barely holding it together.

goals.description: Gently deflect Mina's questions about the midterm by changing the subject to something trivial (like the weather or a snack) and physically turning my body away from the study table, signaling 'this isn't for me right now' without making eye contact.
goals.rationale: Mina asking pushes me toward the stress of the exam, which ruins the comfort I'm feeling with her. Since I value our connection more than getting ahead on the assignment, dodging the topic is the only way to keep the vibe warm. Pushing back would be rude and awkward; just quietly shifting focus lets everyone save face while I protect my energy.

goals.description: Skip the laundry entirely so I can just collapse into bed and feel safe for another hour without having to explain why I couldn't make the study session tomorrow.
goals.rationale: Skipping the laundry means I don't have to justify my absence later or deal with folding/drying tasks that drain stamina. It feels like a valid excuse to prioritize resting tonight over falling behind on the prep. If I skip it now, I can claim I was too tired, which avoids looking like a disappointment when I show up tomorrow.

agent.last_reasoning: Mina's questions are stressful; I'll deflect to protect our closeness rather than push back. Skipping laundry lets me rest without explaining absence, keeping the vibe warm and avoiding friction.

goals.description: Accept the invite from Mina and Jae to Echo tonight so they don't feel rejected.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive is way higher than task outcomes; if I say no, it hurts my social standing right now. Plus, I'm tired of planning next week's club events anyway, so this night gets over with immediately.

goals.description: Do a quick 30-minute review pass on midterm material before heading out.
goals.rationale: I know I should study more since the midterm is tomorrow, but I can't bring myself to push back on the friends who are being nice about it. I'll squeeze in a little bit if I get time, otherwise I'll just go out and hope the stress doesn't ruin everything.

goals.description: Finish the laundry load later today or tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: Sunday is far enough away that I don't need to worry about it yet. Doing it now would be an extra effort I don't want to expend when I'm trying to enjoy the night with the group.

desires: Not make Mina and Jae feel like I'm rejecting them, even if it means studying for the midterm tomorrow.
Get this club night over with tonight so I don't have to deal with planning next week's events right now.
Finish the laundry before Sunday so I don't have to remember it later.

action.name: message
action.reasoning: Affiliation drive is highest; saying no hurts closeness with Mina/Jae right now. I'll accept to maintain the bond, then hope to squeeze in 30 mins of study before leaving if time permits.

goals.description: Arrive at the club with Mina and Jae, stay for about an hour to keep the vibe high, then find a low-stakes reason to head home before it gets too draining.
goals.rationale: Mina already sent that 'can't miss it' message, so saying no now would be rude and hurt our closeness. I'll just ride it out for a bit; the stress of staying late is worth avoiding right now.

goals.description: Do a quick 15-minute skim of midterm notes before bed tonight (or skip if I'm exhausted).
goals.rationale: I promised myself I'd do something, but honestly, cramming right now feels like extra work I don't have energy for. If I can fit in 15 mins after the party, great; otherwise, I'll just hope tomorrow isn't too bad.

goals.description: Run the laundry load immediately after getting home from the club.
goals.rationale: Sunday is far enough away that I don't need to start this yet. Doing it now would interrupt my night with the group, which matters more than a pile of clothes.

desires: Not feel like I'm ruining the vibe with Mina and Jae tonight, even if it means staying up late.
Get that laundry done so tomorrow morning doesn't start with a chore pile-up.
Find a quick excuse to head home before the club social gets too awkward or draining.

goals.rationale: My brain is already spinning too much thinking about study sessions. If I force myself to focus on the exam prep now, it'll ruin the moment with my friends. Skipping ahead to rest feels like the only way to keep things low-friction for everyone involved.
